behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is cross-module handoff (three collectors -> summary input builder). MOCK-MISS applies: a single-function unit test can miss wiring omissions. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real OS/network/subprocess for this specific check (source clients can be in-process fakes), no dependence on model output quality, no multi-agent sequence. Rejected System because behavior text is about "content passed" (payload composition), not real endpoint behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real data-collection/orchestration modules with stubbed source clients returning distinct sentinel fields per source; assert the final payload handed to summary generation contains all three source sections and values.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module interaction boundary: collector error propagation -> report assembler fallback note. Unit is too low because exception handling across modules is the core risk. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess (simulate one source failure in-process), no dependence on LLM judgment, no multi-step agent pipeline. Rejected System because behavior text specifies fallback behavior, not real network timeout semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestration with two healthy source adapters and one adapter that raises an availability error; verify report generation completes and includes a `data unavailable` marker for only the failing section.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on summary content quality (faithfulness to key values), which is model-judgment dependent in a narrative-generation step. Deterministic stubs would hide real misrepresentation/hallucination failures (LLM-DEP). Rejected Workflow because behavior describes a single summary-generation judgment, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use real summary-generation model calls on representative fixtures with known key values; score factual alignment (key metric/value presence and no contradictory claims) across repeated runs with pass/fail thresholds for acceptable variance.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA boundary: this behavior explicitly requires end-to-end delivery to a real inbox, involving real network/email service behavior. Integration fakes can miss SMTP/API/auth/delivery-path failures. Rejected Agentic despite summary step presence because behavior text qualifies success as "reaches ... inbox correctly" (infrastructure delivery outcome), not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled pipeline against reachable test instances/services and a real mailbox; assert one full report email is delivered to configured recipients with expected subject/body structure and no pipeline abort.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Core failure boundary is orchestration/state handoff: send failure -> local persistence -> next-run retry. This is catchable with real modules plus in-process failing email adapter and local filesystem state. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no mandatory real external network needed to validate fallback logic; no LLM-output dependence; no multi-agent sequence. Rejected System because behavior text focuses on retry/fallback state management, not provider-specific transport behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run cycle 1 with email adapter forced to fail; assert report is persisted to local retry store. Run cycle 2 with adapter healthy; assert persisted report is retried/sent and retry artifact is cleared.
